sidebar.trash_empty_button_title: Permanently delete all sessions in trash
sidebar.trash_restore_title: Move this session back to its original workspace
sidebar.trash_purge_title: Permanently remove this session from disk

sidebar.confirm_purge_message: Remove this session from disk. This cannot be undone.
sidebar.deleted_banner_message: Browsing in read-only mode. Restore to continue.

empty_state.subtitle: Claude Code session viewer

empty_state.pick_hint: ← Expand a workspace in the sidebar to pick a session

context_menu.fork_from_here_hint: Claude Code will copy the transcript up to this node into a new jsonl

new_session.title: New session
new_session.cwd_description: Pick from existing project workspaces, or type an absolute path below. The new session uses this as CC's cwd.
new_session.no_workspaces: No workspaces yet. Enter a custom path below.
new_session.custom_path_overrides: Custom path entered — overrides the workspace selection above
new_session.prompt_description: First user message. On submit Loomscope calls the SDK to start a new session with this as the opener.
new_session.prompt_placeholder: What would you like Claude to do?
new_session.err_no_cwd: Pick a workspace or enter a custom path first
new_session.err_no_prompt: v1.6 requires an initial prompt (draft mode coming soon)
new_session.err_absolute_required: cwd must be an absolute path (e.g. /home/user/project)
new_session.err_not_dir: Path exists but is a file, not a directory
new_session.err_unsafe: Directory creation is restricted to under $HOME
new_session.mkdir_confirm_message:
{{path}}

This directory doesn't exist. Run mkdir -p and continue? Cancel to edit the path.

draft_panel.title: Draft session
draft_panel.hint: Nothing said yet — Loomscope hasn't spawned a Claude Code subprocess. Type a message below and press Enter to start it for real.

sidebar_draft.row_title: Placeholder session ({{cwd}}) — Claude Code won't spawn until you send the first message

draft_main.title: Draft session
draft_main.subtitle: Type a message in the panel on the right and hit Enter to spawn the session

session_usage.chip_tooltip: Cumulative token usage for this session. Click for per-turn breakdown + a button to run /cost for the actual dollar figure.
session_usage.breakdown_hint: {{count}} ChatNode(s) with LLM calls (compact / slash-only ChatNodes excluded)
session_usage.cost_explanation: Loomscope doesn't maintain a model pricing table — we can't convert tokens to dollars locally. Click below to send /cost to the active session; CC computes the actual cost and posts it in the conversation.

permission_banner.fallback_headline: Claude Code requests permission to use {{tool}}
permission_banner.viewer_mode: Viewer-only mode — decisions can't be made here; resolve in terminal CC or another writer.

permission_rules.section_description: Loomscope intercepts Claude's tool permission requests via the SDK canUseTool callback. Clicking "Allow always" saves the tool to ~/.loomscope/permissions.json so future calls auto-allow without prompting. Remove rules here.
permission_rules.empty: No rules yet. Click "Allow always" the first time you grant a tool to save one here.

composer.placeholder_input: Type a message… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
composer.priority_now_tooltip: now priority — interrupts the current turn
composer.priority_later_tooltip: later priority — at the tail of the queue
composer.blocked_non_leaf: Selected node isn't this chain's leaf — right-click → "fork from here" to continue.
composer.blocked_offchain: Selected node lives on session {{sid}} — right-click → "jump to source session".
composer.blocked_trashed: This session is deleted — restore to enable input.
composer.blocked_viewer: Viewer-only mode — enable Interactive in Settings → Session runtime to send messages.
composer.status_tokens_in_tooltip: Input tokens (fresh + cache_creation; cache_read replay excluded)
composer.slash_picker_custom_desc: Custom: send any text starting with /
composer.slash_picker_side_effect_hint: Side effect: writes or modifies a file
composer.slash_desc_compact: Compact conversation, keep a summary (optional args to focus the summary)
composer.slash_desc_files: List files touched in this session
composer.slash_desc_heapdump: Dev: write a heap dump (side effect)
composer.slash_compact_tooltip: Run /compact — summarize and truncate context, keeping a summary
composer.slash_compact_confirm_message: /compact clears the conversation history and keeps only a summary as context. The session continues from there; nothing outside the on-disk jsonl is affected.
composer.slash_generic_confirm_message: Send this slash command to Claude Code?
composer.respawning_per_send: Spawning a fresh SDK Query (race protection)
composer.respawning_staleness: External write detected — respawning SDK to sync file state

effective_context.placeholder_no_node: Pick a ChatNode to see the inbound context it actually receives (after auto-compact truncation)
effective_context.intro_with_cutoff: Below is the context this node's LLM call actually sees. Content above the latest compact / hybrid node has been truncated.
effective_context.intro_no_cutoff: No compact in chain — this node sees its full ancestor history.
effective_context.label_compact_summary_hybrid: Compact summary — inline summary inside this hybrid node
effective_context.label_post_compact_tail: Post-compact tail — assistant rounds AFTER the inline compact
effective_context.post_compact_tail_note: Tool pills are omitted here. Switch to the Conversation tab for full tool detail.

git_panel.placeholder_no_node: Pick a ChatNode to see its git commit history
git_panel.placeholder_no_commits: No git commits detected in this turn
git_panel.pending_caveat: = CC-touched files - already-committed files. Re-edits / manual terminal commits / pre-existing dirty are not accurately tracked.

sidebar_search.placeholder_jump: Paste session or node id (Enter to jump)
sidebar_search.tooltip_filter: Filter sessions: type any text or id prefix to narrow the list. Live as you type.
sidebar_search.tooltip_jump: Jump by id: paste a full UUID or 8+ char hex prefix and press Enter. Matches sessions, ChatNodes, and WorkNodes.
sidebar_search.no_results: No id match. Check the prefix or try a longer one.
sidebar_search.truncated_hint: Showing first 10 — narrow the prefix to refine.

placeholders.click_worknode_for_details: Click a WorkNode to see details
placeholders.enter_workflow_first: Enter a workflow first, then pick a WorkNode

stats.chains_tooltip: {{count}} disjoint chains (llm→tool→llm sequences not connected within this ChatNode; typical causes: auto-compact mid-turn, error retry, harness interruption)

running_sessions.label: running
running_sessions.tooltip: v∞.2 SDK sessions with active turns or pending queue
running_sessions.heading: Active sessions

hook_onboarding.title: Loomscope wants to subscribe to Claude Code events
hook_onboarding.body: Loomscope receives 11 CC events (PreToolUse / PermissionRequest / …) via settings.json hooks. Open Settings → Hooks to pick which events to push and to add LOOMSCOPE_SECRET to your shell rc.
hook_onboarding.malformed: Your settings.json is malformed. Fix it manually first, then come back and open Settings to configure hooks.

about.run_in_active_description: Click a button below to send the slash command to the active session; the result appears in the conversation / canvas.
about.cc_version_hint: Claude Code version: run /version in the active session (button below)
about.no_active_session: No active session — pick one from the sidebar, then come back to run.

account.description: Defaults to OAuth credentials from your claude.ai subscription. Toggle the switch below to use ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (per-token API billing) instead.
account.use_api_key_on: Enabled: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env passes through to spawned `claude`; uses API credits.
account.use_api_key_off: Disabled (default): ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is stripped at spawn time; uses subscription.

permissions.description: Maps to `claude --permission-mode` startup flag. Loomscope passes this through when spawning. In `default` mode, SDK non-TTY context silently denies tools that need approval. Pick bypassPermissions to mirror terminal `--dangerously-skip-permissions`. Full canUseTool browser banner (each tool prompts in Loomscope) is on the future backlog.
permissions.mode_default: default — Prompt every time (silent deny in SDK)
permissions.mode_default_hint: Strictest: write tools require approval; non-TTY context (Loomscope) silently denies them.
permissions.mode_acceptEdits_hint: Edit/Write/MultiEdit auto-approved; Bash etc still gated.
permissions.mode_bypassPermissions_hint: Equivalent to --dangerously-skip-permissions; all tool calls allowed.
permissions.mode_plan_hint: Plan mode; tools restricted to read-only.

runtime.mode_description: Interactive (default) gives the full Loomscope experience: composer sends turns, trash can delete & restore, PermissionBanner can allow/deny tool calls. Switching to Viewer-only hides every write affordance (send / trash actions / empty / allow / deny) so Loomscope becomes a read-only browser. Useful for: shared remote view where someone observes but shouldn't steer; public demos; screencasts.
runtime.mode_label: Interactive mode (can send messages, manage trash, approve tools)
runtime.mode_on_hint: Enabled: all write entry points visible.
runtime.mode_off_hint: Disabled (viewer-only): composer / trash actions / permission allow-deny are hidden.
runtime.mode_save_failed: Save failed; check network and retry.
runtime.respawn_description: Claude Code's SDK doesn't lock or tail the underlying jsonl, so when a Loomscope-spawned CC and a terminal CC both append to the same session id, each writes records based on a stale view of the chain — producing duplicate uuids + multi-parent fork artifacts. Enabling this option respawns the SDK Query before every send, so each spawn re-reads the jsonl from disk; race window narrows to sub-second. With it disabled the Query persists (subject to the idle timeout below) and the server only respawns when a pre-send staleness check detects a foreign-writer append. Both modes converge on "always read fresh before write"; the difference is just spawn frequency. ⚠ Known limitation: mid-turn (during takeover) foreign writes can still slip through — neither mode is a 100% race fix. **In practice, avoid running Loomscope and terminal CC on the same session (same sid) at the same time.** See docs/dual-writer-race-mitigation.md for the full rationale.
runtime.respawn_on_hint: Enabled: every send kills the existing Query, spawns a fresh one that reads the current jsonl, then writes. Idle timeout below becomes a "post-turn cleanup bound" rather than a session-lifetime knob.
runtime.respawn_off_hint: Disabled: Query persists across sends; pre-send staleness check (based on jsonl byte-size delta) auto-respawns only when an external write is detected. Lower latency but best-effort blind spots.
runtime.idle_description: After N minutes of inactivity, the server closes that session's SDK Query (kills the `claude` subprocess). The next action respawns; the prompt cache cold-starts.
runtime.idle_range_when_respawn_on: Range 5–240 minutes. With respawn-per-send enabled this only bounds how long an idle-but-still-alive post-turn Query lingers — the next send respawns regardless.

hooks.malformed: settings.json is malformed; refusing to write. Fix the file manually first.
hooks.events_description: When Loomscope isn't running, CC shows a red "hook error" line on every hook fire (non-blocking; chat still works). Tick or untick each event you want pushed to Loomscope; use Select all / none for bulk changes.
hooks.events_paths_note: Note: the checkboxes here only affect the HTTP path (settings.json) — that's the path your terminal-launched claude uses. Loomscope-launched claude additionally uses the Programmatic path below, which IGNORES these checkboxes and sends all events. To truly disable an event, also turn off HTTP path + Programmatic path below.

events.UserPromptSubmit: When the user submits a prompt — drives the card/edge animation start
events.Stop: When the assistant finishes a turn — drives the card/edge animation stop
events.TaskCreated: When a TaskCreate task is created — TaskListPanel updates immediately (without this, falls back to file-watch latency)
events.TaskCompleted: When a TaskCreate task is marked done
events.Notification: When CC fires a system notification (idle / auth / MCP). Wired but no UI consumer yet
events.PermissionRequest: When CC waits for y/n approval — without this the in-browser banner can't show
events.PermissionDenied: When the user denies a permission

hooks.paths_description: You may have two kinds of claude running, each delivering hook events to Loomscope a different way: the claude you launch in your terminal uses the HTTP path; the claude Loomscope launches itself (running behind Loomscope's composer) uses both paths by default for redundancy. Both on is the recommended default — the server dedups automatically; uncheck either to use a single path.
hooks.path_http_label: HTTP path (terminal claude requires this; Loomscope's claude also uses it as a fallback)
hooks.path_http_hint: Routes via settings.json config + LOOMSCOPE_SECRET header auth. Off → the claude you started in your terminal stops reaching Loomscope at all; Loomscope-launched claude still works via the programmatic path below.
hooks.path_sdk_hint: SDK options.hooks JS callbacks delivered in-process (no secret, no settings.json). Only applies to claude instances Loomscope itself launches — terminal claude doesn't use this path. Takes effect on next spawn.
hooks.path_both_off_warning: ⚠ Both off = Loomscope-launched claude reports nothing AND terminal claude is also disconnected (HTTP path is off). Browser sees no PreToolUse / PostToolUse / TaskCreated notifications.
hooks.secret_description: Add this line to ~/.bashrc / ~/.zshrc so CC's hook headers receive the secret.
hooks.rotate_warning: After rotation, every terminal / CC session still holding the old LOOMSCOPE_SECRET will start getting 403. Update the new export line above into ~/.bashrc, reopen your terminal, and restart CC for hooks to resume.

deferral.retry_now_tooltip: Force-resume sending. If Anthropic still rejects, deferral re-arms.
